pages.full_text:
separate vans because it's so cold. Charles & Thomas were difficult. Charles was smacked. We went to bed at 9.30, & slept until 8.30.
__Saturday Oct 3d__
	After separate breakfasts we went on a super 6 mile walk along the valley & through woods. We came to a deep cleft & swing. The boys got filthy. Thomas loved racing around. Lunch at 3, then we played a mega game of foot ball. Late blackberries.
	We booked supper at 7 at the Crown Pub next door. We had lots of choice, including chicken breast & sauces & Tennessee Grasshopper Pie.
__Sunday Oct 4th__.
	Eve grabbed a bowl of cereal & set off with Thomas & Tonic to Church. Simon did breakfast with the others & then some people played cricket.
	It was a 30 minute walk to church along foot paths & roads. A short, family service with a lay-reader. Sermon on revelation. The [illegible] [?1523] of a man The [illegible]
pages.notes: This page continues from a previous page (begins mid-sentence: 'separate vans because it's so cold'). No handwritten page number visible at top-left corner. No year is given explicitly on this page; Oct 3 falling on a Saturday narrows candidates within the 1989–2003 range but year cannot be confirmed from this page alone. The text near the bottom is cut off at the right edge and partially illegible; '1523' may be a hymn number or biblical reference. 'Tonic' may be a dog's name.

pages.full_text:
with 13 sons & 5 daughters. We had coffee & walked home.
	We played cricket & football, blackberried, shared lunch & set off home.
__Saturday October 24th__.
	We set off for lunch with Fatana at __Earl's Court__ via the dog kennel. We met up with Thomas at Fatana's lovely flat. Sarah & Alexander were there. Jeremy was in Hong Kong. The flat is small, black & white, pale floor boards: no carpets. Fatana is about to let it out. It is very well decorated. She took us to a Chinese restaurant, which served delicious food. We all worked hard with chopsticks. It was a lovely meal.
	We then took Thomas's delight to his [h]otel, (his original one had been smashed by burglar) & arrived at the Lee Valley campsite * * * * * We all went to see the 'human show'. (It was so boring that I nearly walked out half-way through). But the idea was not.
__Sunday October 25th__. A pretty disaster-prone day. The wind in the night was [illegible] [illegible]
pages.notes: This page begins mid-sentence continuing from a previous page ('with 13 sons & 5 daughters'). No handwritten page number visible at top-right corner. The Oct 24 and Oct 25 entries appear to be from a different trip/year than the Oct 3–4 entries on the left page (both in October but different dates and context). No year is explicitly stated; Oct 24 falling on a Saturday narrows possibilities within 1989–2003. 'Fatana' is a person's name (owner of the flat at Earl's Court). 'Thomas's delight' — unclear what this refers to; possibly a toy or bicycle that was smashed in a burglary. The 'human show' could be a London theatre or circus show. Text at the bottom is cut off at the page edge. 'Lee Valley campsite' is in the Lea Valley, north-east of London.
